part_title: Section Thirteen: Area-Wide Integrated Pest Management
part_subtitle: Area-Wide Integrated Pest Management of Tuta absoluta

items.text: Chapter Ten
Integrated Management of Tuta absoluta
Introduction

A grower may implement a precise and integrated management program within a field, only for infestation to recur rapidly because moths migrate from an old tomato field, an infested nursery, or abandoned crop residues in the surrounding area.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A grower may implement a precise and integrated management program within a field, only for infestation to recur rapidly because moths migrate from an old tomato field, an infested nursery, or abandoned crop residues in the surrounding area.

items.text: This situation shows that the movement boundaries of Tuta absoluta do not coincide with the boundaries of land ownership.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This situation shows that the movement boundaries of Tuta absoluta do not coincide with the boundaries of land ownership.

items.text: The insect does not stop at:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The insect does not stop at:

items: A change in company or contracting system.
The end of a spray program in one field.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A change in company or contracting system.
[[prepared]]The end of a spray program in one field.

items.text: Areas with overlapping tomato production cycles therefore require collective management targeting the pest population within a defined production landscape.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Areas with overlapping tomato production cycles therefore require collective management targeting the pest population within a defined production landscape.

items.text: Area-wide management does not mean standardizing all operations or spraying every field with the same insecticide. It means coordinating actions that fail when implemented separately by individual producers, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Area-wide management does not mean standardizing all operations or spraying every field with the same insecticide. It means coordinating actions that fail when implemented separately by individual producers, such as:

items: Reducing the green bridge between production cycles.

items.text: Definition of Area-Wide Integrated Pest Management
Area-wide integrated pest management, or:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Area-wide [[term:ipm]]integrated pest management[[/term]], or:

items.text: Area-Wide Integrated Pest Management — AW-IPM
Is the application of an integrated program targeting the entire pest population within a defined geographical area, regardless of boundaries between fields or owners.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Is the application of an integrated program targeting the entire pest population within a defined geographical area, regardless of boundaries between fields or owners.

items.text: This concept differs from conventional management focused on protecting an individual field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This concept differs from conventional management focused on protecting an individual field.

items.text: Conventional Field-Level Management
It asks:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It asks:

items: What is the infestation level in this field?
What is the best treatment for this sector?
prepared_items: [[prepared]]What is the infestation level in this field?
[[prepared]]What is the best treatment for this sector?

items.text: Area-Wide Management
It adds further questions:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It adds further questions:

items: Where are the moths coming from?
Which fields produce the largest numbers of emigrating individuals?
Are nurseries or sorting stations acting as sources?
Is the region repeatedly using one insecticide group?
Can the regional population be reduced before the next production cycle begins?
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Which fields produce the largest numbers of emigrating individuals?
[[prepared]]Are nurseries or sorting stations acting as sources?
[[prepared]]Is the region repeatedly using one insecticide group?
[[prepared]]Can the regional population be reduced before the next production cycle begins?

items.text: Field-level decisions remain important but become part of a wider system.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Field-level decisions remain important but become part of a wider system.

items.text: Why Does Tuta absoluta Require Regional Management?
The regional approach is important because of the following pest characteristics:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The regional approach is important because of the following pest characteristics:

items: Adult insects can move among fields.
Oviposition continues as long as hosts are available.
Multiple generations occur during the year.
Several cultivated and wild hosts are present.
The pest moves with transplants and plant material.
Larvae survive inside fruits and residues.
Moths emerge from fields after harvest has ended.
Old and new production cycles overlap.
Resistance spreads through movement of individuals.
Field edges near infestation sources are highly affected.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Oviposition continues as long as hosts are available.
[[prepared]]Several cultivated and wild hosts are present.
[[prepared]]The pest moves with transplants and plant material.
[[prepared]]Moths emerge from fields after harvest has ended.
[[prepared]]Field edges near infestation sources are highly affected.

items.text: Field-level control alone becomes less sustainable when reinvasion from outside continues.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Field-level control alone becomes less sustainable when reinvasion from outside continues.

items.text: Biological Management Unit
A management area should not be defined only by administrative boundaries, but by the unit within which the pest population moves and host sources are connected.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A management area should not be defined only by administrative boundaries, but by the unit within which the pest population moves and host sources are connected.

items.text: The unit may be:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The unit may be:

items: A connected group of nurseries and fields.
A group of agriculturally connected villages.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A connected group of nurseries and fields.

items.text: The area may extend across more than one administrative district when fields, hosts, and transport operations are interconnected.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The area may extend across more than one administrative district when fields, hosts, and transport operations are interconnected.

items.text: Levels of Spatial Management
Level One: Within the Field
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items.text: Level Two: The Farm
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items.text: Level Three: The Agricultural Block
This includes adjacent fields that exchange moths, equipment, and workers.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes adjacent fields that exchange moths, equipment, and workers.

items.text: Level Four: The Production Region
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items: Transport routes for crops and transplants.

items.text: A program does not need to begin at the scale of an entire governorate. It may start within a manageable production block and expand gradually.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A program does not need to begin at the scale of an entire governorate. It may start within a manageable production block and expand gradually.

items.text: Concept of a Source Field
A source field is a field that produces more pest individuals than it receives and then exports moths to the surrounding area.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A source field is a field that produces more pest individuals than it receives and then exports moths to the surrounding area.

items.text: A field may become a source when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A field may become a source when:

items: It is old and heavily infested.
It remains green after becoming uneconomic.
It contains infested fruits and leaves.
It is removed gradually while living plants remain.
A nearby young crop is attractive to moths.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]It is removed gradually while living plants remain.
[[prepared]]A nearby young crop is attractive to moths.

items.text: A source field need not be the highest-yielding or largest field. A small neglected area may produce large numbers of moths.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A source field need not be the highest-yielding or largest field. A small neglected area may produce large numbers of moths.

items.text: Concept of a Recipient Field
A recipient field receives pest individuals from surrounding sources.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A recipient field receives pest individuals from surrounding sources.

items.text: Young fields are particularly likely to receive moths when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Young fields are particularly likely to receive moths when:

items: They are close to an old field.
They lie downwind from the source.
They lack traps or early monitoring.
Their edges adjoin residues or alternative hosts.
They are planted when the old crop is being removed.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]They are close to an old field.
[[prepared]]They are planted when the old crop is being removed.

items.text: A recipient field may initially have low internal infestation, but external migration pressure accelerates pest establishment.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A recipient field may initially have low internal infestation, but external migration pressure accelerates [[term:establishment]]pest establishment[[/term]].

items.text: Source and Sink Are Not Fixed Attributes
A field’s role may change during the season.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A field’s role may change during the season.

items.text: A new field begins as a recipient, then becomes a source for newer crops after the pest reproduces within it.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A new field begins as a recipient, then becomes a source for newer crops after the pest reproduces within it.

items.text: The sequence is:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The sequence is:

items: 1. Moths move from an old field.
2. Eggs are laid in the new field.
7. The field becomes a new source.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Moths move from an old field.
[[prepared]]2. Eggs are laid in the new field.
[[prepared]]7. The field becomes a new source.

items.text: Repeated cycles maintain the pest throughout the year.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Repeated cycles maintain the pest throughout the year.

items.text: The Green Bridge
The green bridge is the spatial and temporal continuity of plants capable of supporting the pest between seasons.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The green bridge is the spatial and temporal continuity of plants capable of supporting the pest between seasons.

items.text: It consists of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It consists of:

items: Old fields that have not been terminated.
Fruits and residues that allow larvae to continue developing.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Old fields that have not been terminated.
[[prepared]]Fruits and residues that allow larvae to continue developing.

items.text: The more continuous the green bridge, the less effective crop rotation in one individual field becomes.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The more continuous the green bridge, the less effective crop rotation in one individual field becomes.

items.text: Active Movement and Human-Assisted Movement
Active Movement
Moths move among plants and fields while searching for:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Moths move among plants and fields while searching for:

items.text: Movement is affected by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Movement is affected by:

items.text: Passive or Human-Assisted Movement
The pest may be transported with:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The pest may be transported with:

items.text: Human-assisted movement is particularly important between distant areas where local flight alone cannot explain pest arrival.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Human-assisted movement is particularly important between distant areas where local flight alone cannot explain pest arrival.

items.text: Limits of Flight Distance
There is no single universal isolation distance that can be adopted for Tuta absoluta under all environments.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]There is no single universal isolation distance that can be adopted for Tuta absoluta under all environments.

items.text: A fixed distance is difficult to define because of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A fixed distance is difficult to define because of:

items: Difficulty separating active flight from human-assisted movement.
Effects of traps on movement measurements.
Scarcity of direct local field studies measuring dispersal distance.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Difficulty separating active flight from human-assisted movement.
[[prepared]]Scarcity of direct local field studies measuring dispersal distance.

items.text: No specific distance can therefore be claimed to guarantee complete safety.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No specific distance can therefore be claimed to guarantee complete safety.

items.text: The practical risk zone should be defined using:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The practical risk zone should be defined using:

items.text: Aggregated Distribution Within Fields
Spatial studies have shown that Tuta absoluta eggs and damage may have an aggregated rather than uniformly random distribution in open fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Spatial studies have shown that Tuta absoluta eggs and damage may have an aggregated rather than uniformly random distribution in open fields.

items.text: This means that:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This means that:

items: Hotspots may appear before the field average increases.
Edges should be separated from the interior.
Early localized intervention may be possible.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Hotspots may appear before the field average increases.
[[prepared]]Edges should be separated from the interior.

items.text: Aggregated distribution does not mean a hotspot remains fixed. It may expand or new hotspots may appear as females move.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Aggregated distribution does not mean a hotspot remains fixed. It may expand or new hotspots may appear as females move.

items.text: Edges as Warning Zones
Edges adjacent to a potential infestation source are among the first locations that should be inspected.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Edges adjacent to a potential infestation source are among the first locations that should be inspected.

items.text: High-risk edges include those:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]High-risk edges include those:

items: Facing the prevailing wind from the source.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Facing the prevailing wind from the source.

items.text: The following should be compared:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be compared:

items.text: An increase restricted to the edge may indicate external migration before infestation spreads internally.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An increase restricted to the edge may indicate external migration before infestation spreads internally.

items.text: Defining the Operational Area
The area should be defined through practical steps:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The area should be defined through practical steps:

items: 5. Record potato, eggplant, and important alternative hosts.
8. Map transport routes for transplants and produce.
9. Identify old fields and their removal dates.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]5. Record potato, eggplant, and important alternative hosts.
[[prepared]]8. Map transport routes for transplants and produce.
[[prepared]]9. Identify old fields and their removal dates.

items.text: An operational zone should then be drawn to include sources and recipient fields requiring coordination.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An operational zone should then be drawn to include sources and recipient fields requiring coordination.

items.text: Basic Regional Map
The map should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The map should include:

items.text: The map may be prepared using:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The map may be prepared using:

items: An updated paper map where resources are limited.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]An updated paper map where resources are limited.

items.text: The essential requirement is current data, not sophisticated software.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The essential requirement is current data, not sophisticated software.

items.text: Field Register
Each field should receive a permanent code, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each field should receive a permanent code, such as:

items.text: Region – Farm – Field Number – Season
Example:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Example:

items.text: NUB-A07-TOM-2026
The code should be linked to all data on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The code should be linked to all data on:

items.text: Informal names such as “Ahmed’s big field” should be avoided when they could cause confusion in records.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Informal names such as “Ahmed’s big field” should be avoided when they could cause confusion in records.

items.text: Regional Trap Network
The trap network aims to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The trap network aims to:

items: Detect the beginning of activity early.
Determine the direction of migration waves.
Evaluate the effect of old-crop removal.

items.text: The network should not be used to issue one uniform spray decision for all fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The network should not be used to issue one uniform spray decision for all fields.

items.text: The following should be standardized:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be standardized:

items.text: When these elements differ, comparisons among fields become unreliable.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When these elements differ, comparisons among fields become unreliable.

items.text: Warning Traps and Field Traps
Regional Warning Traps
These are placed at strategic locations to measure the general trend, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These are placed at strategic locations to measure the general trend, such as:

items.text: Field Traps
These are used inside each field to monitor local activity.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These are used inside each field to monitor local activity.

items.text: Regional warning traps do not replace field traps because an internal field hotspot may not be evident in a distant regional trap.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Regional warning traps do not replace field traps because an internal field hotspot may not be evident in a distant regional trap.

items.text: Standardizing Trap Reading
Traps should be read on fixed days each week.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Traps should be read on fixed days each week.

items.text: The following should be recorded:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be recorded:

items.text: When exposure periods differ, calculate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When exposure periods differ, calculate:

items.text: Mean daily catch = Total males captured ÷ Number of days the trap was active
A trap operating for seven days should not be compared with one operating for three days using raw counts alone.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A trap operating for seven days should not be compared with one operating for three days using raw counts alone.

items.text: Regional Plant Inspection
Sex-pheromone traps are insufficient because male catches do not directly measure:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Sex-[[term:pheromone-trap]]pheromone traps[[/term]] are insufficient because male catches do not directly measure:

items.text: Indicator or reference fields should therefore be selected in each sector and inspected using a standardized method covering:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Indicator or reference fields should therefore be selected in each sector and inspected using a standardized method covering:

items.text: Reference fields should preferably represent:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Reference fields should preferably represent:

items: Locations near and far from sources.

items.text: Data Units
Measurement units should be standardized.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Measurement units should be standardized.

items.text: Possible units include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Possible units include:

items: Percentage of fruits infested by number.

items.text: Old and new mines should not be combined in a single decision-making value.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Old and new mines should not be combined in a single decision-making value.

items.text: Regional Warning Levels
A multi-level descriptive system may be used.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A multi-level descriptive system may be used.

items.text: Green Level

items: No confirmed plant infestation is present.
No major active sources are present.

items.text: Action:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Action:

items.text: Yellow Level

items: Appearance of eggs or limited hotspots.
Presence of a nearby old field.

items.text: Action:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Action:

items.text: Orange Level
Action:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Action:

items.text: Red Level
Action:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Action:

items: Prevent movement of infested transplants or residues.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Prevent movement of infested transplants or residues.

items.text: The colour categories are levels of organization and response, not uniform spray thresholds.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The colour categories are levels of organization and response, not uniform spray thresholds.

items.text: Issuing the Weekly Bulletin
The bulletin should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The bulletin should include:

items: Catch trend compared with the previous week.
Dates for removal of old fields.
Restrictions related to harvest and residues.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Catch trend compared with the previous week.

items.text: The bulletin should not recommend one commercial product for the entire region without inspecting each field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The bulletin should not recommend one commercial product for the entire region without inspecting each field.

items.text: Speed of Information Flow
Information loses value if it arrives after eggs have become larvae inside fruits.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Information loses value if it arrives after eggs have become larvae inside fruits.

items.text: The system should define:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system should define:

items.text: Possible communication tools include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Possible communication tools include:

items.text: Data-Quality Verification
Data should be reviewed to detect:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Data should be reviewed to detect:

items: Confusion between old and active mines.

items.text: Regional decisions should not be based on unverified data.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Regional decisions should not be based on unverified data.

items.text: Monitoring teams should preferably be trained using:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Monitoring teams should preferably be trained using:

items.text: Regional Crop Calendar
A calendar should show for each week:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A calendar should show for each week:

items.text: The most important risk periods occur when the following coincide:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The most important risk periods occur when the following coincide:

items: A nearby young field is present.

items.text: The calendar can be used to predict migration waves before they occur.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The calendar can be used to predict migration waves before they occur.

items.text: Coordinating Planting Dates
Coordination does not mean planting the entire region on one day. It means reducing overlap that permits continuous pest movement.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Coordination does not mean planting the entire region on one day. It means reducing overlap that permits continuous pest movement.

items.text: The following should be considered:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be considered:

items.text: It may be preferable to establish clear temporal blocks rather than planting a new crop every week beside a field approaching termination.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It may be preferable to establish clear temporal blocks rather than planting a new crop every week beside a field approaching termination.

items.text: Regional Host-Free Period
A host-free period is more effective when it covers most of the region rather than one individual field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A host-free period is more effective when it covers most of the region rather than one individual field.

items.text: It requires removal of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It requires removal of:

items.text: The period is not achieved if infested greenhouses or nurseries remain active without isolation and management.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The period is not achieved if infested greenhouses or nurseries remain active without isolation and management.

items.text: Its duration should be determined according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Its duration should be determined according to:

items.text: No single duration is suitable for all regions.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No single duration is suitable for all regions.

items.text: Protocol for Terminating an Old Field
Termination begins when harvesting is no longer economically viable.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Termination begins when harvesting is no longer economically viable.

items.text: It includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It includes:

items: 4. Avoiding cessation of control while leaving plants alive for a long period.
5. Terminating plants within a short time window.
8. Managing the soil according to the production system.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]4. Avoiding cessation of control while leaving plants alive for a long period.
[[prepared]]5. Terminating plants within a short time window.
[[prepared]]8. Managing the soil according to the production system.

items.text: The effect of termination should be assessed through surrounding traps during the following period.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The effect of termination should be assessed through surrounding traps during the following period.

items.text: Why Should Neighboring Fields Be Notified?
The following operations may cause adult moths to search for a new host:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following operations may cause adult moths to search for a new host:

items.text: When neighboring fields know the timing of these operations, they can:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When neighboring fields know the timing of these operations, they can:

items: Apply an early localized intervention if necessary.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Apply an early localized intervention if necessary.

items.text: Notification does not mean automatic preventive spraying.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Notification does not mean automatic preventive spraying.

items.text: Management of Abandoned Fields
An abandoned field is one in which production or care has stopped while living plants or infested residues remain.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An abandoned field is one in which production or care has stopped while living plants or infested residues remain.

items.text: The regional plan should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The regional plan should include:

items: Provision of shared equipment when necessary.
Regulatory action in large projects or contractual systems.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Regulatory action in large projects or contractual systems.

items.text: The region cannot be protected if highly infested fields remain without clear responsibility.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The region cannot be protected if highly infested fields remain without clear responsibility.

items.text: Nursery Management
Nurseries are central points because one batch can distribute infestation to many fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Nurseries are central points because one batch can distribute infestation to many fields.

items.text: The regional system requires:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The regional system requires:

items: Prohibition of sale of infested transplants.
An inspection certificate or traceability record.

items.text: Each field should be linked to its transplant source.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each field should be linked to its transplant source.

items.text: Traceability of Transplant Batches
Each batch should carry a code identifying:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each batch should carry a code identifying:

items.text: If early infestation appears in several fields supplied by the same batch, the nursery can be identified as a potential source and investigated rapidly.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]If early infestation appears in several fields supplied by the same batch, the nursery can be identified as a potential source and investigated rapidly.

items.text: Transport of Transplants
Transplants should be moved in vehicles that are:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Transplants should be moved in vehicles that are:

items: Not used immediately beforehand to transport infested residues.
Not parked beside old fields for long periods.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Not used immediately beforehand to transport infested residues.
[[prepared]]Not parked beside old fields for long periods.

items.text: Trays should be inspected on arrival and should not enter the field directly when suspicious symptoms are present.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Trays should be inspected on arrival and should not enter the field directly when suspicious symptoms are present.

items.text: Sorting and Collection Stations
Sorting stations may receive infested fruits from multiple fields and accumulate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Sorting stations may receive infested fruits from multiple fields and accumulate:

items.text: The station requires:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The station requires:

items: A closed or controlled waste area.
Prevention of fruit disposal around the site.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Prevention of fruit disposal around the site.

items.text: Risk increases when the station is adjacent to a nursery or young crop.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Risk increases when the station is adjacent to a nursery or young crop.

items.text: Tomato-Processing Factories
Live larvae may reach the factory inside fruits.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Live larvae may reach the factory inside fruits.

items.text: Waste arises from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Waste arises from:

items.text: Untreated waste should not be returned to fields or left in open piles.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Untreated waste should not be returned to fields or left in open piles.

items.text: The factory plan should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The factory plan should include:

items: Anaerobic digestion or another suitable industrial use.
Control of volunteer tomatoes around the site.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Anaerobic digestion or another suitable industrial use.
[[prepared]]Control of volunteer tomatoes around the site.

items.text: Shared Containers
Infested plant material may be transferred with:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infested plant material may be transferred with:

items.text: These should be:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These should be:

items: Prevented from moving directly from old fields to nurseries.
Accompanied by collection of cleaning waste.
Traced by location in advanced systems.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Prevented from moving directly from old fields to nurseries.

items.text: Shared Equipment
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items.text: Movement should be ordered, where possible, from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Movement should be ordered, where possible, from:

items: 3. Then old or highly infested fields.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]3. Then old or highly infested fields.

items.text: Equipment should be cleaned before returning to a young field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Equipment should be cleaned before returning to a young field.

items.text: Cleaning should include conveyors, corners, and areas where leaves and fruits accumulate.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Cleaning should include conveyors, corners, and areas where leaves and fruits accumulate.

items.text: Regional Management of Weeds and Alternative Hosts
One grower may remove hosts from the field while high densities remain on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]One grower may remove hosts from the field while high densities remain on:

items.text: Joint campaigns are therefore needed to identify and remove confirmed hosts.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Joint campaigns are therefore needed to identify and remove confirmed hosts.

items.text: Infested plants should not be removed randomly and left on the ground. They should be collected or treated.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infested plants should not be removed randomly and left on the ground. They should be collected or treated.

items.text: Non-host plants supporting natural enemies should be selectively conserved.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Non-host plants supporting natural enemies should be selectively conserved.

items.text: Regional Management of Natural Enemies
Biological control improves when neighboring fields avoid simultaneous use of highly toxic insecticides.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Biological control improves when neighboring fields avoid simultaneous use of highly toxic insecticides.

items.text: Management includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Management includes:

items: Preventing drift from one field to another.
Establishing sources for recolonization by natural enemies.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Preventing drift from one field to another.
[[prepared]]Establishing sources for recolonization by natural enemies.

items.text: A field using a broad-spectrum insecticide may disrupt a release program in a neighboring field through drift or movement of natural enemies between fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A field using a broad-spectrum insecticide may disrupt a release program in a neighboring field through drift or movement of natural enemies between fields.

items.text: Regional Release of Natural Enemies
Release over a connected area may be more efficient than separate releases when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Release over a connected area may be more efficient than separate releases when:

items: Release points are distributed according to the infestation map.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Release points are distributed according to the infestation map.

items.text: Large-scale release programs require:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Large-scale release programs require:

items.text: A non-native natural enemy should not be released without formal approval and environmental assessment.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A non-native natural enemy should not be released without formal approval and environmental assessment.

items.text: Regional Insecticide-Resistance Management
Resistance develops within a pest population that moves among fields, so rotation within one field alone is insufficient.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Resistance develops within a pest population that moves among fields, so rotation within one field alone is insufficient.

items.text: Management requires:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Management requires:

items: Preventing continuous regional repetition of the same group.
Issuing warnings when reduced susceptibility is confirmed.
Preventing movement of transplants from unmanaged resistance hotspots.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Preventing continuous regional repetition of the same group.
[[prepared]]Issuing warnings when reduced susceptibility is confirmed.
[[prepared]]Preventing movement of transplants from unmanaged resistance hotspots.

items.text: Coordination does not mean every field uses the same active ingredient. It means preventing continuous pressure on one group across the region.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Coordination does not mean every field uses the same [[term:active-ingredient]]active ingredient[[/term]]. It means preventing continuous pressure on one group across the region.

items.text: Difficulty of Standardizing Insecticide Windows
Fields differ in:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fields differ in:

items.text: A single insecticide schedule therefore cannot be issued for all fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A single insecticide schedule therefore cannot be issued for all fields.

items.text: Coordination is achieved by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Coordination is achieved by:

items: Identifying groups that should not be repeated.
Avoiding use of one group against successive generations across the region where possible.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Identifying groups that should not be repeated.
[[prepared]]Avoiding use of one group against successive generations across the region where possible.

items.text: Edge Management
In some situations, monitoring or intervention may be directed to high-risk edges instead of treating the entire field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]In some situations, monitoring or intervention may be directed to high-risk edges instead of treating the entire field.

items.text: Edge management includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Edge management includes:

items: Releasing parasitoids when eggs are present.
Applying selective localized treatment when infestation is confirmed.
Monitoring the direction of mine spread.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Applying selective localized treatment when infestation is confirmed.

items.text: Edge treatment should not be calendar-based when there is no evidence of migration or establishment.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Edge treatment should not be calendar-based when there is no evidence of migration or establishment.

items.text: Response to a New Regional Hotspot
When a sudden increase appears in a specific area:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When a sudden increase appears in a specific area:

items: 3. Increase inspection density around the hotspot.
6. Inspect sorting stations and waste sites.
10. Apply an integrated intervention in the hotspot.
12. Issue a targeted warning rather than an exaggerated general alert.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]3. Increase inspection density around the hotspot.
[[prepared]]6. Inspect sorting stations and waste sites.
[[prepared]]10. Apply an integrated intervention in the hotspot.
[[prepared]]12. Issue a targeted warning rather than an exaggerated general alert.

items.text: Definition of a Regional Outbreak
There is no single global number defining a regional outbreak.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]There is no single global number defining a regional outbreak.

items.text: Operationally, it is identified through the convergence of several indicators, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Operationally, it is identified through the convergence of several indicators, such as:

items: Increased trap catches in multiple sectors.
Infestation in fields of different ages.

items.text: An outbreak should not be declared from one high trap count without plant inspection.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An outbreak should not be declared from one high trap count without plant inspection.

items.text: Regional Emergency Plan
The emergency plan should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The emergency plan should include:

items.text: The plan should focus on reducing the source population rather than increasing uncoordinated spraying.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The plan should focus on reducing the source population rather than increasing uncoordinated spraying.

items.text: Institutional Responsibilities
Grower

items: Avoid selling infested transplants or materials.

items.text: Agricultural Engineer
Nursery
Factory or Sorting Station
Cooperative or Organizing Company
Regulatory Authorities
Technical Leadership of the Program
The program requires one clearly defined coordinating body.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The program requires one clearly defined coordinating body.

items.text: Its responsibilities include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Its responsibilities include:

items.text: The program fails when several organizations issue contradictory instructions without a single technical reference.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The program fails when several organizations issue contradictory instructions without a single technical reference.

items.text: Data Sharing and Confidentiality
Growers may hesitate to report infestation because of concerns about:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Growers may hesitate to report infestation because of concerns about:

items.text: The system should therefore:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system should therefore:

items: Present general maps without unnecessarily identifying growers.
Distinguish technical reporting from deliberate non-compliance.
Prevent unfair use of data against cooperating growers.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Present general maps without unnecessarily identifying growers.
[[prepared]]Prevent unfair use of data against cooperating growers.

items.text: Concealing a hotspot harms the entire region.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Concealing a hotspot harms the entire region.

items.text: Economic Incentives
A grower may bear the cost of removing an old field while most of the benefit accrues to neighboring growers.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A grower may bear the cost of removing an old field while most of the benefit accrues to neighboring growers.

items.text: This creates the problem of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This creates the problem of:

items.text: Individual cost versus collective benefit
Possible solutions include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Possible solutions include:

items: Linking delivery acceptance to compliance with records.
A shared fund for hotspot management.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Linking delivery acceptance to compliance with records.

items.text: Collective action cannot rely on awareness alone when economic incentives are misaligned.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Collective action cannot rely on awareness alone when economic incentives are misaligned.

items.text: Cost of Non-Cooperation
Lack of cooperation by a limited number of fields may lead to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Lack of cooperation by a limited number of fields may lead to:

items: Movement of the pest into new production cycles.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Movement of the pest into new production cycles.

items.text: These costs should be quantified using regional records.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These costs should be quantified using regional records.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Operational indicators include:

items: Percentage of traps read on time.
Percentage of old fields removed on schedule.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Economic indicators include:

items.text: Before-and-After Comparison
Performance should be compared with a baseline established before regional management begins.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Performance should be compared with a baseline established before regional management begins.

items.text: Weekly Dashboard Template

rows: Percentage of fields with active mines

items.text: Source-Field Report Template
Technical Warning Message Template
Warning level: Orange

Repeated increases in male Tuta absoluta catches were recorded this week in the eastern sector, coinciding with the beginning of removal of several old tomato fields and the appearance of fresh eggs on the edges of two newly transplanted fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Repeated increases in male Tuta absoluta catches were recorded this week in the eastern sector, coinciding with the beginning of removal of several old tomato fields and the appearance of fresh eggs on the edges of two newly transplanted fields.

items.text: Required actions:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Required actions:

items: Notify the team before removing any old field.
Do not conduct a general spray based on traps alone.
Make decisions for each field according to inspection and dominant life stage.
Review the IRAC group used in the previous window.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Notify the team before removing any old field.
[[prepared]]Do not conduct a general spray based on traps alone.
[[prepared]]Make decisions for each field according to inspection and dominant life stage.
[[prepared]]Review the IRAC group used in the previous window.

items.text: Artificial Intelligence and Damage Recognition
Image-analysis applications may classify leaves or estimate damage severity, but limitations include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Image-analysis applications may classify leaves or estimate damage severity, but limitations include:

items: Similarity of mines to other damage.
Inability to consistently distinguish active from old mines.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Inability to consistently distinguish active from old mines.

items.text: These tools should support inspectors rather than replace full field diagnosis.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These tools should support inspectors rather than replace full field diagnosis.

Temperature data help estimate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Temperature data help estimate:

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It is preferable to use:

items: A model validated in the region.

items.text: Regional spray decisions should not be based on degree-days alone but integrated with traps and field inspection.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Regional spray decisions should not be based on degree-days alone but integrated with traps and field inspection.

items.text: Mapping Regional Resistance Management
Each field may be linked to the most recent IRAC group used, producing a map showing:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each field may be linked to the most recent IRAC group used, producing a map showing:

items.text: The map helps prevent continued pressure from one group within the same sector.

items.text: Commercially sensitive details should not be disclosed without appropriate data protection.

items.text: Seasonal Review
At the end of the season, the review should ask:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]At the end of the season, the review should ask:

items: What were the principal infestation sources?
When did the first wave begin?
Did the trap network perform adequately?
Which chemical groups were most used?
What was the percentage of infested fruits?
What was the cost of the program?
prepared_items: [[prepared]]What was the percentage of infested fruits?
[[prepared]]What was the cost of the program?

items.text: The results should be converted into clear modifications for the following season.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The results should be converted into clear modifications for the following season.

items.text: Common Errors
Error One: Treating Every Field as an Independent Unit
The pest moves among fields and facilities.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The pest moves among fields and facilities.

items.text: Error Two: Standardizing the Spray Decision Across the Entire Region
Infestation, life stage, and crop stage differ among fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infestation, life stage, and crop stage differ among fields.

items.text: Error Three: Relying on Traps Without Plant Inspection
Male catch alone does not measure damage.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Male catch alone does not measure damage.

items.text: Error Four: Comparing Counts from Different Trap and Lure Types
This weakens the validity of comparisons.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This weakens the validity of comparisons.

items.text: Error Five: Reading Traps at Irregular Intervals Without Adjusting Exposure Duration
This produces misleading numbers.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This produces misleading numbers.

items.text: Error Six: Neglecting Nurseries
They may distribute infestation over a wide area.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They may distribute infestation over a wide area.

items.text: Error Seven: Leaving Old Fields After Control Stops
They become migration sources.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They become migration sources.

items.text: Error Eight: Removing Fields Without Notifying Neighbours
This may cause them to miss the timing of early inspection.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This may cause them to miss the timing of early inspection.

items.text: Error Nine: Managing Waste Within Fields but Neglecting Sorting Stations or Factories
The source may shift outside the field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The source may shift outside the field.

items.text: Error Ten: Establishing a Host-Free Period on One Farm Only
It fails if hosts continue in the surrounding region.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It fails if hosts continue in the surrounding region.

items.text: Error Eleven: Standardizing the Insecticide Instead of Coordinating Modes of Action
This may increase regional resistance.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This may increase regional resistance.

items.text: Error Twelve: Declaring an Emergency from One High Trap Count
Spread should be confirmed through plant inspection.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Spread should be confirmed through plant inspection.

items.text: Error Thirteen: Collecting Data Without an Organization to Analyze Them
Unused data do not provide an early warning.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Unused data do not provide an early warning.

items.text: Error Fourteen: Delayed Warning Issuance
The message may arrive after larvae have entered the plant.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The message may arrive after larvae have entered the plant.

items.text: Error Fifteen: Penalizing Early Reporting
This encourages growers to conceal hotspots.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This encourages growers to conceal hotspots.

items.text: Error Sixteen: Ignoring Economic Incentives
A grower may not implement an action benefiting neighbours if bearing the full cost alone.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A grower may not implement an action benefiting neighbours if bearing the full cost alone.

items.text: Practical Priorities in Egypt
The priorities are:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The priorities are:

items: 1. Define production blocks for processing and fresh-market tomatoes.
2. Register fields, nurseries, and crop schedules.
4. Select reference fields for plant inspection.
6. Identify old fields before control stops.
9. Regulate waste from sorting stations and factories.
12. Inventory solanaceous hosts along edges and drains.
13. Protect natural enemies at the production-block level.
15. Establish a system for reporting insecticide failure.
16. Collect resistance samples from high-risk areas.
17. Link technical compliance to supply contracts.
18. Provide incentives to growers who remove source fields.
20. Evaluate the program by fruit infestation and cost, not trap counts alone.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Define production blocks for processing and fresh-market tomatoes.
[[prepared]]2. Register fields, nurseries, and crop schedules.
[[prepared]]4. Select reference fields for plant inspection.
[[prepared]]6. Identify old fields before control stops.
[[prepared]]9. Regulate waste from sorting stations and factories.
[[prepared]]12. Inventory solanaceous hosts along edges and drains.
[[prepared]]13. Protect natural enemies at the production-block level.
[[prepared]]15. Establish a system for reporting insecticide failure.
[[prepared]]16. Collect resistance samples from high-risk areas.
[[prepared]]17. Link technical compliance to supply contracts.
[[prepared]]18. Provide incentives to growers who remove source fields.
[[prepared]]20. Evaluate the program by fruit infestation and cost, not trap counts alone.

items.text: Scientific Summary of Section Thirteen
Tuta absoluta moves among fields, nurseries, greenhouses, sorting stations, and factories. The boundaries of the pest population therefore do not coincide with the boundaries of land ownership.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta absoluta moves among fields, nurseries, greenhouses, sorting stations, and factories. The boundaries of the pest population therefore do not coincide with the boundaries of land ownership.

items.text: Area-wide management aims to target the pest population within an interconnected geographical area while field-level intervention decisions continue to be based on actual field conditions.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Area-wide management aims to target the pest population within an interconnected geographical area while field-level intervention decisions continue to be based on actual field conditions.

items.text: The principal components of regional management include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The principal components of regional management include:

items.text: Spatial studies have shown that Tuta absoluta eggs and damage have an aggregated pattern in open fields, supporting the value of mapping, hotspot inspection, and edge monitoring.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Spatial studies have shown that Tuta absoluta eggs and damage have an aggregated pattern in open fields, supporting the value of mapping, hotspot inspection, and edge monitoring.

items.text: There is no single isolation distance that guarantees prevention of pest movement. Risk zones should instead be defined through traps, inspection, host distribution, field age, and movement routes of transplants and residues.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]There is no single isolation distance that guarantees prevention of pest movement. Risk zones should instead be defined through traps, inspection, host distribution, field age, and movement routes of transplants and residues.

items.text: Old fields in which harvesting or control has stopped while plants remain alive are among the most important sources of reinvasion of young crops.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Old fields in which harvesting or control has stopped while plants remain alive are among the most important sources of reinvasion of young crops.

items.text: Nurseries, sorting stations, and factories are also central points capable of spreading the pest when traceability, sanitation, and containment are absent.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Nurseries, sorting stations, and factories are also central points capable of spreading the pest when traceability, sanitation, and containment are absent.

items.text: Collective action does not mean standardizing insecticides or implementing regional spraying. It means coordinating shared actions while preserving field-level decisions based on monitoring.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Collective action does not mean standardizing insecticides or implementing regional spraying. It means coordinating shared actions while preserving field-level decisions based on monitoring.

items.text: The program requires clear technical leadership, standardized data, economic incentives, rapid communication, and indicators measuring infestation, yield, cost, and resistance.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The program requires clear technical leadership, standardized data, economic incentives, rapid communication, and indicators measuring infestation, yield, cost, and resistance.
